subject: Physics
title: Fermi's Chicago Pile-1
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Fermi's Chicago Pile-1.

hook.text: On December 2, 1942, Enrico Fermi created the first controlled nuclear chain reaction under a football stadium.

buildup.text: Chicago Pile-1 was built from 40,000 blocks of graphite and uranium, stacked by hand in a squash court.

discovery.text: When cadmium control rods were slowly withdrawn, the neutron count rose steadily—the chain reaction was self-sustaining.

twist.text: There was no radiation shielding. If the reaction went out of control, a man stood ready to chop the rope holding the safety rod.

climax.text: Fermi let it run for 28 minutes, then shut it down. The atomic age had begun with a quiet click on a counter.

punchline.text: Graphite blocks and a squash court. The first chain reaction. Controlled.

quiz.question: Where was the first controlled nuclear chain reaction achieved?
options: Under a football stadium at the University of Chicago
